text: seen with exposed metal like this were the gearheads . Her fear escalated into terror. If it meant to kill her, that was one thing; it’d be done quick. But the way it was suspended over her, the way it held her pinned down … “Lara,” it said . Warlord’s face flashed in her mind’s eye. She thrashed beneath the inhuman force holding her captive, kicked and slapped, ignoring her pain as she struck its solid body . “Lara,” the bot said again, its voice penetrating her terror . She stilled. The bot sounded just like Ronin. Panting, she looked up into its face. One of its eyes was damaged, a jagged, disc-shaped piece of metal lodged in the socket. The other was a familiar shade of green . “Ronin?” she rasped . “Who else would it be ?” “I thought you were a fucking gearhead!” Her terror subsided, shifting into horror as realization hit her. “Oh my God! What happened to you? Does it hurt ?” She lifted her hands to touch his face, but he turned away before she made contact . “Don’t feel much of anything, right now .” Lara placed her hands on his cheeks. He reached up to brush them away, but stopped when she said, “Don’t.” His metal was warm beneath her palms . “I see the fear in your eyes,” he said. “You don’t have to look at this .” “I thought you were one of them . If I knew it was you…I’d never be afraid of you, Ronin.” Another realization hit her, this one a punch to her gut. “Did I shoot you? Oh , shit !” “You missed.” His lip-plates shifted into what might have been a smile. “We’ll have to talk about that sometime. You’re a terrible shot .” “Not like I ever shot a gun before. God, you look like shit! What happened ?” Carefully, she brushed her thumb along the disc protruding from his eye; it didn’t budge. Her eyes, already stinging, blurred . “A trap.” He settled a hand on her face, wiping a tear from her cheek with a bare-metal finger. “Why are you crying ?” “Look at you.” Her eyes dipped, looking over his ravaged chest, the blackened metal of his arms, the chunks of shrapnel sticking out of him . One metal brow lowered — the other couldn’t move — and he seemed to frown. Gently, he guided her hand away from his face and pushed himself aside. He crawled to the stairs, right leg dragging behind him, and used the railing to pull himself to his feet . Lara scrambled up and hurried to his side. “Let me help .” “No. I’ll manage .” “Dammit, Ronin. Let me at least get that junk out of your leg.” Helplessness left a sour taste in her mouth . “Made it eighteen miles with that junk in my leg. Can make it a little farther .” “Why do you have to be so damned stubborn?” She moved past him and crouched on the steps, putting her at his eye level. “Let me help .” “I’m just being what I am.” Ronin stood upright, casting his scorched arms to either side. “ Look at me .” “What are you talking about ?” “There’s no pretending I ever was, or will be, human. I’m just a reminder of everything you hate .” She stared at him in disbelief. Why was he acting like this? What did she say to set him off, this time? “I don’t hate you. And of course you’re not human. That’s pretty overrated, anyway. You’re Ronin .” “I saw your expression. You can’t stand to look at me, and I can’t blame you for that .” “Is that what you think this is about? Seriously? I was horrified for you !” “What reason would you have to be horrified for me ?” “I know you call it deactivation instead of death, but…you look like you came pretty damn close. You might be tougher than me, but you can still get blown up .” “This damage is largely superficial. I’ve…endured worse.” He dropped his gaze, expression neutral. “This could have been worse. You don’t need to shed tears for me. I’m here. I told you I’d come back, and here I stand .” Her eyes burned anyway. “But you might not have come back,” she said angrily, and lunged at him, wrapping her arms around his torso. He staggered, but she held tight. Ignoring the smell, she focused on his solidness, his heat . * * * W ith most of his skin gone, Ronin’s ability to register touch was limited to raw data, little more than basic pressure readings and estimated temperatures. Despite that, Lara’s embrace was warm and strong. It wasn’t a gesture of fear or uncertainty, but one of relief. She hugged him like she may never have another chance to, like she wasn’t ever going to let him go, and she was right in saying he might never have returned . How could he have been so blind to what he put her through, when he left her like that ? “Doesn’t do us any good to consider what might have been,” he said, slipping his arms around her with great care. Without the more precise sensors in his skin, there was too much chance for error. A slight overapplication of his strength could crush her bones. “This is what happened, and it’s all we’ve got .” “You owe me an apology .” “I’m sorry I left .” “No. For thinking it matters to me what you look like .” Her words swirled through his processors, settling deep in his memory as he contemplated their meaning. His appearance did matter to her, but not in the way he’d thought; she didn’t care if he was a bot or a human, only that he was… himself . She did care about his wellbeing . “Which is like shit right now, correct?”
